implemented: Real NAB data downloaded from Kaggle (58 datasets, 22,695 rows)
Real NAB evaluation: AUC 0.9776, Neural Loss 3.44, Baseline 1.17
Anomaly calibration diagnosed: threshold too strict (0/567 detected)
Test suite optimized: fast/slow tiers with conftest.py markers
finding: On real NAB data, the neural model achieves AUC 0.9776 (well above 0.9 T1 threshold) but recall is 0 — the anomaly detection threshold is too conservative. The model can rank anomalies correctly but the warn/block thresholds need to be relaxed. Neural prediction loss (3.44) is worse than baseline persistence (1.17), confirming that stable experts own the forecast floor.
decision: T1 support confirmed for NAB (AUC > 0.9). Next: fix anomaly calibration to improve recall.
